item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always have time for crustaceans with an appreciation for fabulous plunder.

I mean, coconut crabs of any kind are just wonderful, but this one is shiny enough to warrant an immediate upvote.

Honestly, this is an underrated SCP. It actually is one of my favorites on this site (which is saying a lot). How are there only 19 ratings? This is not only the funniest not -J I've ever read, but really well done. Good job writer! Keep it up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like it but i think more can be done with this concept then guy ran off to find gold with crab .. could we get a foundation funded voyage with the crab as the navigator.. have them run in to some open sea SCPs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, they're not different at all. It doesn't make any sense. And "answar" doesn't parse immediately as "answer but with an a". The pirate speech could come across without overreliance on phonetic spelling, just having him say 'me' instead of 'my' pretty much did all the work for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How do they know he's saying 'aye' and not 'I', for one?

I was trying to write it in such a way that anyone reading this out loud would be forced to sound like a pirate. The syllables of 'Aye' and 'I' are different when you sound them out, so I played up the pirateness as much as possible in the interview. I can see why such a thing would break your immersion, especially since the Foundation is the one writing this Log. If more people say the same, I'll probably go ahead and fix it. And yes, I am aware that 'Aye' means 'yes' in "pirate-speak".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was growing on me as a goofy, off-kilter kind of comedy skip, but then this

It be alright with me, so long as aye can refuse to answar at any time.

knocked me right out of it. How do they know he's saying 'aye' and not 'I', for one?

item.title: Here it is
item.content:encoded:
This is just something I've been working on throughout the day for jamcon 2018. If this article doesn't work out, I'm probably going to quit the wiki for a while.

Image credit:
